---
title: "`r knitr::current_input()`"
date: "`r paste('last updated', 
    format(lubridate::now(), ' %d %B %Y'))`"
output:   
  html_document:  
        code_folding: show
        toc: true 
        toc_depth: 2
        toc_float: true
  pdf_document:   
    latex_engine: xelatex  
    toc: true
    toc_depth:  2   
    fontsize: 11pt   
    geometry: margin=0.5in,top=0.25in   
---

##  Collection simple vector examples.

```{r setup, include=FALSE		}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE,
                      comment = "      ##",
                      error = TRUE,
                      collapse = FALSE   ) # easier to read
library(jimTools)
```

### Create atomic vector, slice and dice:
```{r create}
v  <- 1:1000
head(v)

# first 100
first  <- v[1:100]
first

# rest 
rest  <- v[-(1:100)]
rest
head(rest)
```

### Use cut, split - FAIL!
*  cut   is used to create intevals?  (factors?) 
*  split is used to chop vector based on a FACTOR
```{r cut}
```
### using vector()
  *   asq
  *  `mode`  think base class or list
https://rdrr.io/r/base/vector.html
```{r vector}
vector(mode="logical", length=0)

# logical of length 
v  <- vector(mode="logical", length=0) 
v

# logical of length 1
v1   <- vector(mode="logical", length=1) 
v1

# logical of length 2
v2   <- vector(mode="logical", length=2) 
v2


# list of length 5, values are NUL
l1  <- vector(mode="list", length=5)
l1

```






```{r knit_exit(), include = FALSE  , eval=FALSE } 
# knitr::knit_exit()
```



```{r render, eval=FALSE, include=FALSE 	} 

# TODO:  file is found when knitr runs (see above)

# file must be of form:
# dir/name_of_this_file    where dir is relative to project root

file  <- "0010_base_vectors.Rmd"
file  <- here("vector", file)
file

# in general, pdf will look nicer
rmarkdown::render(file,
                  #output_format = "pdf_document",
                  output_format = "html_document",
                  output_file = "~/Downloads/print_and_delete/out")
```
